nodes.text: Hi. Picking up a prescription. Diane Yoo.

responses.text: I'll look you up. Can I see your ID?
Do you have a moment? I need to verify a few things.

nodes.text: Yeah, fine. It's the levothyroxine. Fifty micrograms. Been on it since—  hold on. *checks phone* Sorry. What do you need?

responses.text: Just your ID and date of birth.
How long have you been on levothyroxine?

nodes.text: Great. I'm also gonna grab the nail files over here — I know where they are, I'm not going to knock anything over.

responses.text: Go ahead. I'll process your prescription.

nodes.text: Here's the ID. DOB is on it. *phone buzzes* One second — okay. Okay. Sorry, what were you saying?

responses.text: I have what I need. You're all set once I run this through.

nodes.text: God. Like four years? Dr. Blake caught it on a routine panel. I was tired all the time and I thought it was just the job but no, turns out my thyroid's just slow. *checks phone* Sorry.

responses.text: Nothing to apologize for. Let me get your ID and we'll get you out of here.
